etymology.description: śąą(śąą) (gńď, 'spear') + śąą(śąą) (gńď, 'spear') = two spears facing each other -> original form of śģė(śģė) (c√°n, 'to damage')
etymology.interpretation: The character depicts two spears facing each other symbolizing conflict or destruction, which evolved as the original form of śģė(śģė) (c√°n, 'to damage').
etymology.simplification: Simplified to śąč by reducing the number of strokes, depicting just simplified straight lines instead of complete spear forms.
